question: When preparing to drive to a new location, you:

question: Which types of medication can potentially impair your driving ability?

question: What action should drivers take when a pedestrian hybrid beacon is not illuminated?

question: What does it mean when you see double solid yellow lines on the road?

question: Is it ever permissible to drive around or underneath a railroad crossing barrier?

answers.text: Under no circumstances.
answers.explanation: Driving around or under a lowered railroad crossing gate is illegal under any circumstance. Always wait until the gate is fully raised and you are sure the tracks are clear.

answers.text: Only if the gate is malfunctioning.
If the crossing lights are not illuminated.

explanation: Driving around or under a lowered railroad crossing gate is illegal under any circumstance. Always wait until the gate is fully raised and you are sure the tracks are clear.

question: What should you do when you spot animals alongside the road while driving?

answers.text: Increase your speed to pass them quickly.
Reduce your speed and carefully approach.
answers.explanation: If you observe animals close to the road, decrease your speed and move forward with caution, as they could suddenly dart into your path. Often, there are additional animals nearby that may also move onto the roadway.

answers.text: Suddenly change lanes to avoid them.

explanation: If you observe animals close to the road, decrease your speed and move forward with caution, as they could suddenly dart into your path. Often, there are additional animals nearby that may also move onto the roadway.

question: When you encounter a funeral procession on the road, you:

question: During poor weather conditions with limited visibility, what should you do?

question: How should you respond when a school bus is stopped with flashing red lights?

answers.text: You may proceed only when children are not visible.
You must stop until the red lights stop flashing.
answers.explanation: When a school bus has stopped and its red lights are flashing, you must stop your vehicle at least 20 feet away and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing or you are signaled to proceed. This rule applies even on divided highways in New York State.

answers.text: You can pass only if you approach from the opposite direction.
You may pass if you see the front of the bus.

explanation: When a school bus has stopped and its red lights are flashing, you must stop your vehicle at least 20 feet away and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing or you are signaled to proceed. This rule applies even on divided highways in New York State.

question: Which of the following does not affect your blood alcohol content (BAC)?

answers.text: The amount of alcohol you consume.
The interval of time between drinks.
Your body mass.
The type of alcohol you consume.
answers.explanation: Blood alcohol content (BAC) is based on how much alcohol you've had, the time between drinks, and your body weight. It is not influenced by the type of alcoholic drink you had.

explanation: Blood alcohol content (BAC) is based on how much alcohol you've had, the time between drinks, and your body weight. It is not influenced by the type of alcoholic drink you had.

question: What is the only way to become sober enough to drive safely after drinking?

question: To make a left turn from a one-way street to another one-way street, you should begin from:

answers.text: The far-left lane.
answers.explanation: When making a left turn from a one-way street onto another one-way street, initiate the turn from the far-left lane. Be cautious of pedestrians, motorcyclists, and bicyclists who may also occupy this lane for their turns. Turn into any available lane that is safely open.

answers.text: The middle lane.
Any lane that appears to be open.

explanation: When making a left turn from a one-way street onto another one-way street, initiate the turn from the far-left lane. Be cautious of pedestrians, motorcyclists, and bicyclists who may also occupy this lane for their turns. Turn into any available lane that is safely open.

question: Which method is effective in eliminating alcohol from your body?

answers.text: Allowing time to pass is the only way to sober up completely.
answers.explanation: The liver processes alcohol at a constant rate, and nothing can speed up this process. It takes about one hour to metabolize one standard drink.

answers.text: Drinking coffee.
Exercising to sweat it out.

explanation: The liver processes alcohol at a constant rate, and nothing can speed up this process. It takes about one hour to metabolize one standard drink.
